basics.name: Joe Pegler
basics.label: Full-stack engineer · Transaction infrastructure · SDKs
basics.summary: Full-stack engineer with production experience across backend, frontend, mobile, and SDK systems, with a current focus on transaction infrastructure and execution. I build from 0 to 1 across product and platform layers, from UI and mobile surfaces to backend pipelines and developer tooling. Blockchain infra across EVM and Solana.

selectedImpact: Built and maintain Statecraft, an open-source TypeScript SDK for deterministic Ethereum integration testing with explicit scenario fixtures and isolated chain runtimes.
Shipped an in-house transaction bundler with retry logic, simulation, and structured error decoding, enabling stable gasless and sponsored flows.
Led the TypeScript SDK at Biconomy for smart accounts and multi-chain execution: modern signing (EIP-712/1271, passkeys), production integrations, and clear developer experience.
Built defi.app execution infrastructure: multi-chain quote and route orchestration, DeFi action providers, and EVM plus Solana simulation and sponsorship systems.
Shipped 0→1 product surfaces, from dApp and intents execution at Enso to automated CI/CD for trading interfaces at CoinFLEX.

work.name: defi.app
work.summary: Own transaction infrastructure and developer tooling for a unified cross-chain DeFi product.
highlights: Designed and shipped an internal transaction bundler with retries, simulation, and Sourcify plus ABI-backed error decoding to increase delivery reliability across product-facing execution flows.
Built and published Statecraft as an open-source SDK to make Ethereum integration tests deterministic through typed scenario fixtures, pinned chain state, and per-file isolated runtimes.
Built cross-chain quote and route orchestration across LiFi, Jupiter, 1inch, Odos, Mayan, LayerZero, and Relay to expose one execution surface instead of chain-specific flows.
Owned the TypeScript SDK (RouteClient and ExecutionManager) for smart accounts, quote, and execution paths, reducing integration load for frontend and backend product teams.
Delivered EVM plus Solana simulation and sponsorship infrastructure, including Jito-aware validation, enabling sponsored transactions across both ecosystems.

work.name: biconomy
work.summary: Owned the core developer SDK for smart accounts and multi-chain execution.
highlights: Built the flagship TypeScript SDK from scratch and led architecture plus API design; as of Apr 2026 the package (@biconomy/abstractjs) records 7,652 weekly and 29,868 monthly downloads.
Implemented EIP-712 and EIP-1271 signing with P-256 passkey support to enable keyless and gasless UX patterns.
Primary contributor to the open-source SDK repository with 97 commits, establishing the core architecture and initial implementation across account and execution flows.
Shipped viem-based modular clients with Safe support, cutting complexity for account and bundler integration paths.
Hardened production readiness with typed errors, composable batching, and CI coverage with Vitest, Bun, GitHub Actions, and Codecov.

work.name: enso
work.summary: Led 0 to 1 delivery of Enso's client dApp and execution stack.
highlights: Owned end-to-end delivery of the primary Ethereum dApp, from frontend experience to backend execution, while requirements and product scope were still moving.
Maintained the Graph protocol indexer used by the dApp and backend for on-chain data.
Shipped and extended intents plus solver infrastructure for decentralized trade execution, enabling intent-driven UX and competitive solver paths.

work.name: coinflex
work.summary: Led the trading interface and deployment system for the live exchange product.
highlights: Led delivery of the platform's trading UI, owning architecture, core feature rollout, and engineering standards across a small team.
Built fully automated CI/CD for the interface stack, reducing release risk and supporting faster iteration on a live trading product.

earlierExperience: GameAnalytics - Lead Frontend Developer (2016 to 2019): led a team of five, set stack and quality practices, awarded company MVP 2018/19.
FSI, Healthcare 21, and Nixatel (2013 to 2016): delivered cross-platform enterprise products and 0 to 1 mobile plus web systems in small teams.
